tasks.subject: [form-field-standardization] [P1] Install Shadcn Field and export from shared barrel
tasks.description:
Install the Shadcn Field component and add all exports to the shared UI barrel.

## Steps

1. Run the Shadcn CLI to install the Field component:

```bash
cd apps/client && pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add field
```

This creates `apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/field.tsx` with 10 sub-components: `Field`, `FieldLabel`, `FieldDescription`, `FieldError`, `FieldContent`, `FieldTitle`, `FieldGroup`, `FieldSet`, `FieldLegend`, `FieldSeparator`.

2. Verify the generated file exists at `apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/field.tsx` and contains all 10 exports. The Shadcn configuration in `apps/client/components.json` uses:
   - Style: `new-york`
   - Base color: `zinc`
   - Aliases: `components` → `@/layers/shared/ui`, `utils` → `@/layers/shared/lib/utils`
   - Icon library: `radix`

3. Add all Field exports to `apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/index.ts`. Insert the following block in alphabetical order among existing exports (after the `DropdownMenu` exports and before `HoverBorderGradient`):

```tsx
export {
  Field,
  FieldContent,
  FieldDescription,
  FieldError,
  FieldGroup,
  FieldLabel,
  FieldLegend,
  FieldSeparator,
  FieldSet,
  FieldTitle,
} from './field';
```

4. Run `pnpm typecheck --filter=@dorkos/client` to verify no type errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/field.tsx` exists and exports all 10 components
- All 10 components are re-exported from `apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/index.ts`
- `pnpm typecheck --filter=@dorkos/client` passes
- `pnpm lint --filter=@dorkos/client` passes
tasks.activeForm: Installing Shadcn Field and updating barrel exports

tasks.subject: [form-field-standardization] [P2] Migrate ToolsTab ToolBlockSection to SettingRow
tasks.description:
Replace the inline flex row inside the `ToolBlockSection` private component in `ToolsTab.tsx` with `SettingRow`, handling the compound children case (Badge + Switch). ## File `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/ToolsTab.tsx` ## Current ToolBlockSection (lines 81-120) The inner row pattern at lines 94-112: ```tsx

{description}

{!available && ( {unavailableReason} )}
``` ## After Replace the inner `
` block with: ```tsx
{!available && ( {unavailableReason} )}
``` Note: The `space-y-0.5` on the inner div differs slightly from `SettingRow`'s `FieldContent` styling. Verify visually that the gap between label and description looks identical. If `FieldContent` defaults don't match, adjust by adding a className override. ## Import Changes Add `SettingRow` to imports from `@/layers/shared/ui`: ```tsx import { Badge, Label, Switch, SettingRow } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Remove `Label` from the import if no longer used directly. ## Acceptance Criteria - `ToolBlockSection` inner row uses `` instead of inline flex divs - Compound children (Badge + Switch) wrapped in a flex container as children - `pnpm typecheck --filter=@dorkos/client` passes - No visual regression — Badge and Switch still align correctly on the right

tasks.subject: [form-field-standardization] [P2] Migrate ContextTab and CapabilitiesTab to SettingRow
tasks.description:
Replace inline settings row patterns in the agent-settings feature's `ContextTab.tsx` and `CapabilitiesTab.tsx`. ## File 1: ContextTab.tsx `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/ContextTab.tsx` The `ContextBlockSection` private component (lines 71-110) has the same structure as `ToolBlockSection` in ToolsTab. Replace the inner flex row: **Before (lines 83-102):** ```tsx

{description}

{!available && ( {unavailableReason} )}
``` **After:** ```tsx
{!available && ( {unavailableReason} )}
``` Add `SettingRow` to imports: ```tsx import { Badge, Label, Switch, SettingRow } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Remove `Label` if no longer used directly. ## File 2: CapabilitiesTab.tsx `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/CapabilitiesTab.tsx` The `ToolGroupRow` private component (lines 51-115) has two render paths: ### Server-disabled path (lines 59-78) **Before:** ```tsx

{domain.description}

...
``` **After:** ```tsx ... ``` ### Normal path (lines 81-114) **Before:** ```tsx

{domain.description}

... {isOverridden && ...}
``` **After:** ```tsx
... {isOverridden && ...}
``` ### Core Tools row (lines 377-385) This row at line 377 also matches the pattern but has no Switch — it has a text label "Always enabled" instead. Use SettingRow: ```tsx Always enabled ``` Add `SettingRow` to imports: ```tsx import { Badge, Button, Label, Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue, Switch, Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipTrigger, SettingRow, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Remove `Label` if no longer used directly in the file (check Capabilities, Namespace, Budget, Tool Groups labels). ## Acceptance Criteria - `ContextBlockSection` inner row uses `` - Both paths of `ToolGroupRow` use `` - Core Tools row uses `` - `pnpm typecheck --filter=@dorkos/client` passes - Existing agent-settings tests pass (`pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/`) - No visual regression

tasks.subject: [form-field-standardization] [P2] Migrate TunnelDialog and PersonaTab to SettingRow
tasks.description:
Replace inline settings row patterns in `TunnelDialog.tsx` and `PersonaTab.tsx`. ## File 1: TunnelDialog.tsx `apps/client/src/layers/features/settings/ui/TunnelDialog.tsx` The bottom toggle (lines 478-481): **Before:** ```tsx
Enable remote access
``` This row is a simplified pattern — it has a label but no description. It does NOT match `SettingRow` since `description` is a required prop. Leave this row as-is, OR if the spec intends for it to be migrated, add a description. Per the spec, this file has "1" pattern for auth toggle at bottom. Looking at the actual code, the toggle at line 478 is "Enable remote access" with no description — this is a borderline case. **Decision:** Leave as-is since it lacks a description and doesn't cleanly match the SettingRow API. The spec says `TunnelDialog.tsx` has 1 pattern for "Auth toggle", but the actual code shows a simple enable/disable toggle without a description text. ## File 2: PersonaTab.tsx `apps/client/src/layers/features/agent-settings/ui/PersonaTab.tsx` The toggle row at line 89: **Before:** ```tsx
``` This row has a label but no description text. Like TunnelDialog, it doesn't cleanly match the `SettingRow` API which requires both `label` and `description`. Leave as-is unless we want to add a description. **Decision:** Leave as-is. Both of these are label-only rows without descriptions. They could use `` directly for accessibility benefits, but that's a lighter touch: ```tsx Inject persona into sessions ``` Apply this `Field` + `FieldLabel` pattern to PersonaTab's toggle row and TunnelDialog's enable toggle for the accessibility benefit (`aria-describedby` association) even without a description. ## PersonaTab Migration Add Field imports: ```tsx import { Field, FieldLabel, Switch, ... } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Replace: ```tsx
``` With: ```tsx Inject persona into sessions ``` ## TunnelDialog Migration Add Field imports and replace the enable toggle similarly. ## Acceptance Criteria - PersonaTab toggle row uses `` + `` for accessibility - TunnelDialog enable toggle uses `` + `` for accessibility - `pnpm typecheck --filter=@dorkos/client` passes - Existing tests pass - No visual regression
tasks.activeForm: Migrating TunnelDialog and PersonaTab to Field primitives

tasks.subject: [form-field-standardization] [P4] Add SettingRow and PasswordInput showcases to dev playground
tasks.description:
Add SettingRow and PasswordInput showcase sections to the dev playground Forms page. ## File 1: Extend ComposedFormShowcases `apps/client/src/dev/showcases/ComposedFormShowcases.tsx` Add two new section components and render them in the main export: ```tsx import { useState } from 'react'; import { PlaygroundSection } from '../PlaygroundSection'; import { ShowcaseLabel } from '../ShowcaseLabel'; import { ShowcaseDemo } from '../ShowcaseDemo'; import { TimezoneCombobox } from '@/layers/features/pulse/ui/TimezoneCombobox'; import { ScanRootInput } from '@/layers/features/mesh/ui/ScanRootInput'; import { SettingRow, PasswordInput, Switch, Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue, Button, Badge, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; export function ComposedFormShowcases() { return ( <> ); } // ... existing sections ... // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- // SettingRow // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- function SettingRowSection() { const [switchValue, setSwitchValue] = useState(false); const [selectValue, setSelectValue] = useState('option1'); const [compoundSwitch, setCompoundSwitch] = useState(true); return ( With Switch control
With Select control
With destructive Button control
With Badge + Switch (compound children)
Relay disabled
); } // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- // PasswordInput // --------------------------------------------------------------------------- function PasswordInputSection() { const [defaultValue, setDefaultValue] = useState('secret123'); const [visibleValue, setVisibleValue] = useState('my-api-token'); const [sentinelValue, setSentinelValue] = useState('***'); return ( Default (hidden)
setDefaultValue(e.target.value)} />
With visibleByDefault
setVisibleValue(e.target.value)} />
Sentinel mode (clears on focus)
setSentinelValue(e.target.value)} onFocus={() => setSentinelValue('')} placeholder="Saved — enter a new one to replace" showPassword={false} />
); } ``` ## File 2: Update forms-sections.ts `apps/client/src/dev/sections/forms-sections.ts` Add two new entries to the `FORMS_SECTIONS` array at the end (in the Composed category): ```tsx { id: 'settingrow', title: 'SettingRow', page: 'forms', category: 'Composed', keywords: ['setting', 'row', 'horizontal', 'field', 'switch', 'toggle', 'label', 'description'], }, { id: 'passwordinput', title: 'PasswordInput', page: 'forms', category: 'Composed', keywords: ['password', 'input', 'eye', 'toggle', 'visibility', 'sentinel', 'secret'], }, ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - SettingRow showcase shows 4 variants: Switch, Select, destructive Button, Badge+Switch compound - PasswordInput showcase shows 3 variants: default hidden, visibleByDefault, sentinel mode - Both sections appear on the Forms page under "Composed" category - TOC sidebar shows both new entries - `pnpm typecheck --filter=@dorkos/client` passes
tasks.activeForm: Adding SettingRow and PasswordInput showcases to dev playground

tasks.subject: [form-field-standardization] [P4] Update design system docs and run final validation
tasks.description:
Add a "Form Fields" section to `contributing/design-system.md` documenting the new components, then run full typecheck and lint. ## File `/Users/doriancollier/Keep/dork-os/core/contributing/design-system.md` Add a new section (choose appropriate location — after input/form-related sections or at the end before references): ```markdown ## Form Fields DorkOS uses [Shadcn Field](https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/radix/field) as the foundation for all form field layouts. Two higher-level components sit on top. ### SettingRow Horizontal settings row — label and description on the left, control on the right. Use for all settings toggle/select patterns. ```tsx import { SettingRow, Switch } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` For compound controls (e.g., Badge + Switch), wrap them in a flex container: ```tsx
Disabled
``` ### PasswordInput Password input with eye/eye-off visibility toggle. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes. ```tsx import { PasswordInput } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; // Uncontrolled (manages its own visibility state) // Start visible // Controlled visibility ``` Sentinel mode ("saved value" placeholder that clears on focus) is a consumer concern — pass `onFocus`, `placeholder`, and `value` props. ### Field Orientation Conventions | Context | Orientation | Component | |---|---|---| | Settings rows | `horizontal` | `` | | Wizard/form fields | `vertical` | `` | | Responsive layouts | `responsive` | `` | For rows that have a label but no description (e.g., simple toggles), use `` + `` directly instead of `SettingRow`. ``` ## Final Validation Run the full suite to catch any issues across the entire migration: ```bash pnpm typecheck pnpm lint pnpm test -- --run ``` Fix any issues found. ## Acceptance Criteria - `contributing/design-system.md` has a "Form Fields" section documenting SettingRow, PasswordInput, and Field orientation conventions - `pnpm typecheck` passes across all packages - `pnpm lint` passes across all packages - `pnpm test -- --run` passes across all packages - No `Label` imports remain in files that were migrated to `FieldLabel` or `SettingRow` (unless `Label` is still needed for other purposes in that file)
tasks.activeForm: Updating design system docs and running final validation
